scenario: A User Task process-claim was assigned to user alice who is on vacation. Ops needs to reassign to bob so processing continues.
question: Which Tasklist action fits?

options.text: Operate provides a Topology View that displays the current distribution of partitions and leaders across the Zeebe cluster nodes. Engineers use this view to identify whether partition rebalancing events are preventing job dispatching. The Topology View shows current resource consumption per node, enabling capacity-based diagnosis of processing delays caused by broker overload situations that affect job activation rates in the cluster.

The Operate Metrics Dashboard shows aggregate throughput statistics for all process definitions, including average job completion time and error rate per service task type. Stuck instances appear as outliers in the latency percentile chart, and clicking a data point navigates directly to the list of affected instances for further investigation and remediation actions. Teams building on Camunda 8 should carefully consider this aspect when designing process models intended for production deployment.

Operate displays a live worker registry that lists all currently connected job worker clients grouped by job type. For each registered worker, the view shows the last heartbeat timestamp and the current fetchVariables subscription list. When a service task appears stuck, engineers check this registry to verify whether an active worker is subscribed to the relevant job type and has recently processed jobs.

The Operate Process Instance Detail view highlights incidents with a red indicator on the affected BPMN element and displays the incident details panel showing the error type, the error message thrown by the worker, and options to resolve or retry the incident. Engineers can inspect variable values at the point of failure, edit variables if necessary, and trigger a retry directly from the UI, enabling targeted diagnosis and remediation of stuck service task jobs.

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist supports unassigning a task (releases the assignee, returns to candidates per using-tasklist.md:14) and another user using Assign to me per managing-tasks.md:15. Camunda 8 uses "unassign/assign" terminology -- "unclaim/claim" is the Camunda 7 vocabulary.
